entities.name: Talent Agent Economy
entities.context: Proposed paradigm shift in how talent is represented and verified, moving away from traditional hiring infrastructure

entities.name: Superunicorn
entities.context: Term for exceptionally valuable individual employees who become critical assets as companies shrink

entities.name: Mental Model
entities.context: Verified representation of a person's actual capabilities, curated by talent agents

entities.name: Scouting Report
entities.context: Curated evidence of talent's capabilities, managed by agents similar to sports/entertainment

entities.name: Resume
entities.context: Traditional hiring artifact described as architecturally incapable of finding true talent

entities.name: ATS
entities.context: Applicant Tracking System - part of current infrastructure that only finds people good at appearing competent

entities.name: Credential
entities.context: Traditional verification method deemed inadequate for identifying actual competence

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Platform where elite managed talent will no longer be found

entities.name: Representation Model
entities.context: Sports and entertainment approach where elite talent has agents rather than applying for roles

relationships.context: The Talent Agent Economy applies the sports/entertainment representation model to tech talent
Resumes are architecturally incapable of identifying Superunicorns
ATS systems fail to find actual high performers, only those who appear competent
Both are curated artifacts that agents manage to verify and represent talent
Superunicorns are the elite assets that will be managed through the new talent agent model
Traditional credentials cannot identify true Superunicorn talent
